mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases net present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate environment is accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2020-01-15 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and capital formation. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both RSAFS and HOUST to show month-over-month growth simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations and predicts positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive 1-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439891M and HOUST at 1001K as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances where both proxies show simultaneous growth, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized rate signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as higher production translates to earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerating to below 50K new jobs per month signals weakening labor demand and foreshadows reduced consumer spending. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets price in demand weakness.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, a real rate compression occurs that incentivizes equity risk-taking and predicts positive 20-day returns. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both monetary accommodation and inflation pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.08 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2020-01-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized signals late-cycle weakness and foreshadows earnings compression. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when the economy enters a sub-trend growth regime, as equity valuations compress in response to reduced profit growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining more than 10% month-over-month signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets anticipate spillover effects to consumer spending and construction employment.
regime_hint: demand shock or housing market correction
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window following trigger activation.
